title: baccharis glabrata
intro: Baccharis glabrata, also known as the dwarf silver broom or silver broom, is a shrub native to South America and is a member of the asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and treat infections. The medicinal properties of Baccharis glabrata have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Baccharis glabrata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
